source.title: Statin Use for the Primary Prevention of Cardiovascular Disease in Adults: Clinical Summary of the USPSTF Recommendation

signals: Grade: B and Grade: C recommendations
adults 40 years or older who do not already have CVD

extraction.summary: The USPSTF recommends statin therapy for primary prevention of cardiovascular disease in adults aged 40-75 years with cardiovascular risk factors. For those with 10-year CVD risk ≥10%, statins should be initiated (Grade B). For those with 7.5-9.9% risk, statins should be selectively offered (Grade C).

population.risk_criteria: One or more cardiovascular risk factors: dyslipidemia, diabetes, hypertension, or smoking
population.disease_definition: Primary prevention of cardiovascular disease in adults without existing CVD
population.exclusions: Adults with existing CVD or signs/symptoms of CVD; LDL cholesterol >190 mg/dL; known familial hypercholesterolemia
population.severity_staging: 10-year CVD risk stratification: ≥10% (high risk), 7.5-9.9% (intermediate risk), <7.5% (lower risk)

key_thresholds.context: Primary target population for statin consideration

recommendations.recommendation: Initiate a statin for adults aged 40-75 years with cardiovascular risk factors and 10-year CVD risk ≥10%
recommendations.evidence_level: Moderate certainty of moderate net benefit
recommendations.notes: Should discuss rationale with patient and obtain agreement

recommendations.recommendation: Selectively offer a statin for adults aged 40-75 years with cardiovascular risk factors and 10-year CVD risk 7.5-9.9%
recommendations.evidence_level: Moderate certainty of small net benefit
recommendations.notes: Consider patient values and preferences; benefit is smaller

recommendations.recommendation: Insufficient evidence to recommend for or against starting a statin in adults ≥76 years
recommendations.notes: Evidence is insufficient to assess balance of benefits and harms

red_flags: Existing cardiovascular disease or signs/symptoms of CVD
extraction.follow_up: Not specified - refer to standard lipid monitoring guidelines
extraction.escalation_path: Patients with LDL >190 mg/dL or familial hypercholesterolemia should be referred to specialized hypercholesterolemia management guidelines from other organizations
